title: Battery cell chemistry change: P-1062 LiPo → LiFePO4
text:
Engineering Change Request ECR-214.

Raised by: Lena Okafor (Battery & Power Systems Engineer), 2023-10-12.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-10-19.

Summary: Change the battery cell assembly (P-1062) from LiPo (6S2P 22.2V 20Ah) to LiFePO4 chemistry.

Rationale: The K-200 experienced a LiPo thermal runaway event on 8 September 2023 during a hot-day endurance test (KES-208). This was the second thermal anomaly on P-1062 cells (the first was a cell swelling event in March 2023). LiPo cells present an unacceptable risk under IEC 62133-2 compliance requirements for BVLOS maritime operations, particularly given the salt-water submersion risk profile. The customer contract (ref KES-208) explicitly prohibits LiPo chemistry for BVLOS maritime operations.

LiFePO4 chemistry offers superior thermal stability (stable to 270°C vs LiPo thermal runaway at ~150°C) at a cost of approximately 12% lower energy density. The endurance reduction from 75 minutes projected to 66 minutes is within the 60-minute minimum requirement. Trade study presented at WIKI-052 (Battery Chemistry Design Review, 2023-10-05).

Impact:
- P-1062: New LiFePO4 cell assembly (CellCore Technologies, 6S2P 22.2V 20Ah LiFePO4)
- P-1075: BMS module charge/discharge profile must be updated for LiFePO4 voltage curves (3.2V nominal vs 3.7V LiPo)
- P-1064: Battery connector to remain AS150 — no change
- DOC-421: Battery System Specification to be updated to Rev C
- P-1079: Battery wiring harness — no change required
- Qualification testing: Full cell-level and pack-level qualification per IEC 62133-2 required before ECN issue

Related: KES-208 (thermal runaway bug), WIKI-052 (design review meeting), DOC-421 (battery spec update).

sections.body:
ECR-214 | Battery cell chemistry change: P-1062 LiPo → LiFePO4

Engineering Change Request ECR-214.

Raised by: Lena Okafor (Battery & Power Systems Engineer), 2023-10-12.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-10-19.

Summary: Change the battery cell assembly (P-1062) from LiPo (6S2P 22.2V 20Ah) to LiFePO4 chemistry.

Rationale: The K-200 experienced a LiPo thermal runaway event on 8 September 2023 during a hot-day endurance test (KES-208). This was the second thermal anomaly on P-1062 cells (the first was a cell swelling event in March 2023). LiPo cells present an unacceptable risk under IEC 62133-2 compliance requirements for BVLOS maritime operations, particularly given the salt-water submersion risk profile. The customer contract (ref KES-208) explicitly prohibits LiPo chemistry for BVLOS maritime operations.

LiFePO4 chemistry offers superior thermal stability (stable to 270°C vs LiPo thermal runaway at ~150°C) at a cost of approximately 12% lower energy density. The endurance reduction from 75 minutes projected to 66 minutes is within the 60-minute minimum requirement. Trade study presented at WIKI-052 (Battery Chemistry Design Review, 2023-10-05).

Impact:
- P-1062: New LiFePO4 cell assembly (CellCore Technologies, 6S2P 22.2V 20Ah LiFePO4)
- P-1075: BMS module charge/discharge profile must be updated for LiFePO4 voltage curves (3.2V nominal vs 3.7V LiPo)
- P-1064: Battery connector to remain AS150 — no change
- DOC-421: Battery System Specification to be updated to Rev C
- P-1079: Battery wiring harness — no change required
- Qualification testing: Full cell-level and pack-level qualification per IEC 62133-2 required before ECN issue

Related: KES-208 (thermal runaway bug), WIKI-052 (design review meeting), DOC-421 (battery spec update).

closure.title: Battery cell chemistry change: P-1062 LiPo → LiFePO4
closure.summary:
Impact/dependency closure for ECR-214 (Battery cell chemistry change: P-1062 LiPo → LiFePO4) [formal].
Impacted if changed (23 records, <=3 hops): ECN-312 (Approved Change — ECR-214: Battery cell ), ECN-314 (Approved Change Notice for ECR-214), DOC-421 (K-200 Battery System Specification v2.0 ), DOC-430 (K-200 Systems Maintenance Procedure), KES-208 ([Bug] LiPo thermal runaway on P-1062 dur), WIKI-052 (Battery Chemistry Design Review — 2023-1), WIKI-062 (Action Items: Integration Milestone 4), REV-650 (Review of DOC-421: REV-650), KES-002 ([Epic] Propulsion system integration), WIKI-070 (Action Items: Systems Milestone 4), SRC-003 (power/bms_monitor.py — Battery), KES-137 ([Story] Create verification matrix), KES-070 ([Bug] Mission file import validation err), KES-088 ([Story] Integrate interface control docu), KES-093 ([Story] Update supplier data package), KES-109 ([Story] Qualify software design document), KES-162 ([Story] Verify ICD rev B), KES-180 ([Story] Implement power budget model), KES-218 ([Story] Integrate maintenance manual sec), ECR-216 (Payload gimbal redesign: reject GimbalTe), ECN-313 (Approved Change — ECR-216: Payload gimba), WIKI-038 (Payload Gimbal Design Review — 2024-06-1), SRC-006 (gnc/gnc_common.py — Nav/GNC).
Depends on (30 records, <=3 hops): P-1062 (Battery Cell Assembly — LiFePO4), P-1075 (BMS Module), P-1064 (Battery Connector — Main), P-1079 (Battery Wiring Harness), DOC-421 (K-200 Battery System Specification v2.0 ), KES-208 ([Bug] LiPo thermal runaway on P-1062 dur), KES-210 ([Bug] Mission file import validation err), WIKI-052 (Battery Chemistry Design Review — 2023-1), DRW-510 (DRW MD-DRW-510: BMS Enclosure Assembly), DRW-532 (DRW MD-DRW-532: BMS to Bus Wiring), DRW-515 (DRW MD-DRW-515: Power Distribution Wirin), DRW-503 (DRW MD-DRW-503: Battery Bay Assembly), P-1076 (Power Distribution Board), ECN-312 (Approved Change — ECR-214: Battery cell ), ECR-209 (BMS P-1075 safety parameter update: SOC ), DOC-431 (K-200 BMS Safety Specification v1.3), REV-623 (Review of DOC-431: BMS Safety Specificat), P-1061 (Battery Enclosure), KES-019 ([Epic] Integration and test environment ), P-1080 (BMS Heatsink), P-1081 (BMS Enclosure), P-1082 (Overvoltage Protection Module), P-1077 (Current Sensor), P-1072 (Main Fuse 50A), P-1063 (Battery Enclosure Lid), P-1067 (Battery Mounting Bracket), P-1017 (Wiring Harness Bracket — Airframe), WIKI-014 (BMS Safety Architecture Review — 2023-07), DRW-521 (DRW MD-DRW-521: Battery Retention System), DOC-434 (K-200 Battery Qualification Test Report).
